source.name: Neon Postgres Documentation
source.sections: 17 (introduction, get-started, connect, local, cli, ai, auth, data-api, branching, manage, guides, import, workflows, reference, postgresql, security, extensions)
source.format: Markdown available at .md suffix per page
source.update-frequency: Weekly — Neon releases features on a regular cadence
source.plugin: neon-postgres@neon (installed) — provides CLI and DB tools but not doc crawling

rate-limits.known-limits: No published crawl rate limits. Docs are Vercel/CDN-served.
rate-limits.decision: Slightly more conservative than claude-docs because larger page count (244 vs 84). Full crawl takes ~6 min at standard rate. Sample crawl first.

processing.content-extraction: Fetch .md suffix → extract title from first "# " line

decisions.decision: Neon docs inform our SDK primitives for Neon Postgres integration. Each of our 18 repos has a Neon database — understanding the docs is critical for branch-per-PR workflows and neonctl usage.
We already have the neon-postgres plugin installed. The crawler complements this by tracking doc changes that affect our SDK primitives over time.
